description: Example of the RAW data the Thales SDK produces for one QS2000 passport scan, captured from the SDK's level-5 debug log (Reader.EnableLogging / bridge --debug-log flag). All values are FICTIONAL but internally consistent: the MRZ lines parse, and every check digit below is correctly computed per ICAO 9303. Use this to understand what exists upstream of the WebSocket contract (bridge/CONTRACT.md); fields marked NOT forwarded never reach clients today.
notes_on_raw_date_format: The SDK logs dates as DD-MM-YY (e.g. 14-05-90 = 14 May 1990). The bridge windows 2-digit years: DOB past-biased, expiry future-biased — see bridge/README.md.

raw_images_produced.CD_IMAGEVIS: full visible-light page scan, JPEG, typically 300-500 KB (mapped to images.front)
raw_images_produced.CD_IMAGEPHOTO: cropped face photo, JPEG, typically ~100 KB (mapped to images.portrait)
raw_images_produced.IR_UV_images: not produced — disabled in Application.ini; QS2000 has no IR/UV illumination

chip_rfid: when no RFID data items arrive, the contract frame carries chip: null

fields_in_raw_but_NOT_in_contract.puOptionalData1: MRZ line-2 optional data — can carry a national identifier on some passports (e.g. Indonesia). Not forwarded by the bridge today.
fields_in_raw_but_NOT_in_contract.puCodeline1_puCodeline2: the raw MRZ lines themselves — useful for audit/re-parsing. Not forwarded.
fields_in_raw_but_NOT_in_contract.puCodelineValidationResult: the SDK's overall MRZ check-digit verdict — a trust signal clients never see. Not forwarded.
fields_in_raw_but_NOT_in_contract.raw_check_digit_results: per-field check digit pass/fail — not forwarded.
fields_in_raw_but_NOT_in_contract.puDocCode: raw ICAO document code (P, ID, ...) — only the normalized documentType is forwarded.
